name: Evan Hull
headline: Camp body in Houston after another lost, inefficient season
body:
The advanced numbers explain why Hull keeps bouncing around rosters. Across six games in New Orleans last year he managed just 19 carries for 48 yards, a brutal 2.53 per attempt, with only 1.05 yards after contact and a -0.378 EPA per play that ranked him 111th. He cleared 19.2 percent of snaps just once and posted a lone reception, finishing at 5.4 fantasy points all season. There is no breakaway juice here: zero runs of 10-plus yards, zero broken tackles, and a long gain of eight.

Now on his third team in three years, Hull arrives in Houston after an April waiver claim and projects as a depth piece battling for table scraps behind the established backfield. He carries no real ADP and sits near the bottom of the running back board. Until an injury opens a path to touches, he is a preseason watch-list name and nothing more for redraft purposes.
